to_generate.name: version_number
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_VERSION_NUMBER C preprocessor macro resolves to an integer with the value (X*1000000 + Y*1000 + Z) where X, Y, and Z are the same numbers used in SQLITE_VERSION.

to_generate.name: version
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_VERSION C preprocessor macro in the sqlite3.h header evaluates to a string literal that is the SQLite version in the format "X.Y.Z" where X is the major version number (always 3 for SQLite3) and Y is the minor version number and Z is the release number.

to_generate.name: sqlite3_libversion

to_generate.name: sqlite3_sourceid

to_generate.name: sqlite3_libversion_number

to_generate.name: compileoption_used

to_generate.name: compileoption_get

return result ? String(result) : String();
to_generate.doc: Returns the N-th compile time option string. If N is out of range, returns an empty String. The SQLITE_ prefix is omitted from any strings returned by this function.

to_generate.name: sqlite3_threadsafe
to_generate.doc: Returns zero if and only if SQLite was compiled with mutexing code omitted due to the SQLITE_THREADSAFE compile-time option being set to 0.

Each open SQLite database is represented by a pointer to an instance of the opaque structure named "sqlite3". It is useful to think of an sqlite3 pointer as an object. The sqlite3_open(), sqlite3_open16(), and sqlite3_open_v2() interfaces are its constructors, and sqlite3_close() and sqlite3_close_v2() are its destructors.

to_generate.name: close
to_generate.doc: Closes a database connection. Returns SQLITE_OK if the sqlite3 object is successfully destroyed and all associated resources are deallocated.

to_generate.name: close_v2
to_generate.doc: Closes a database connection. Returns SQLITE_OK regardless of the state, but marks the connection as a zombie and deallocates it later when all prepared statements are finalized, all BLOB handles are closed, and all backups have finished.

to_generate.name: initialize
to_generate.doc: Initializes the SQLite library. This routine is designed to aid in process initialization and shutdown on embedded systems. Workstation applications using SQLite normally do not need to invoke this routine as it is called automatically by other SQLite interfaces.

to_generate.name: shutdown
to_generate.doc: Deallocates any resources that were allocated by sqlite3_initialize(). This routine is designed to aid in process initialization and shutdown on embedded systems. Workstation applications using SQLite normally do not need to invoke this routine. All open database connections must be closed and all other SQLite resources must be deallocated prior to invoking this routine.

to_generate.name: os_init
to_generate.doc: Performs operating-system specific initialization of the SQLite library. This routine is called automatically by sqlite3_initialize(). Typical tasks include allocation or deallocation of static resources, initialization of global variables, setting up a default sqlite3_vfs module, or setting up a default configuration using sqlite3_config().
Defines the interface between SQLite and low-level memory allocation routines. Used with sqlite3_config() to specify alternative memory allocation subsystems for SQLite.

to_generate.name: os_end
to_generate.doc: Undoes the effect of sqlite3_os_init(). This routine is invoked indirectly by sqlite3_shutdown(). Typical tasks include deallocation of static resources, cleanup of global variables, and tearing down the default sqlite3_vfs module.

to_generate.name: config_serialized

to_generate.name: total_changes64

to_generate.name: interrupt
to_generate.doc: Causes any pending database operation to abort and return at its earliest opportunity. Typically called in response to a user action such as pressing 'Cancel'.

to_generate.name: is_interrupted
to_generate.doc: Returns true if an interrupt is currently in effect for the database connection.

to_generate.name: complete
to_generate.doc: Determines if an SQL statement is complete. Returns 1 if the input string appears to be a complete SQL statement, 0 if incomplete, or SQLITE_NOMEM if a memory allocation fails.

to_generate.name: complete16
to_generate.doc: UTF-16 version of complete(). Determines if an SQL statement is complete. Input must be a zero-terminated UTF-16 string in native byte order.

to_generate.name: busy_handler
to_generate.doc: Sets a callback to handle SQLITE_BUSY errors. The callback receives the number of previous invocations as an argument and returns 0 to stop retrying or non-zero to retry.

to_generate.name: busy_timeout
to_generate.doc: Sets a busy handler that sleeps for a specified amount of time when a table is locked. Calling with ms ≤ 0 turns off all busy handlers.

to_generate.name: setlk_timeout
to_generate.body:
ERR_FAIL_COND_V(db.is_null() || !db->is_valid(), SQLITE_ERROR);
return sqlite3_setlk_timeout(db->handle, ms, flags);
to_generate.doc: Sets the setlk timeout for blocking locks on WAL-mode databases. Only available in SQLITE_ENABLE_SETLK_TIMEOUT builds. Passing 0 disables blocking locks, -1 requests indefinite blocking.

to_generate.name: setlk_block_on_connect_flag
to_generate.doc: Flag for sqlite3_setlk_timeout(): block on connect when the number of connections falls from 1 to 0.

to_generate.name: get_table
to_generate.doc: Executes one or more semicolon-separated SQL statements and returns the result as a 2D array. The first row contains column names. This is a legacy interface; use of this interface is not recommended.

to_generate.name: free_table
to_generate.doc: Frees memory allocated by sqlite3_get_table(). Must only be called with a result pointer obtained from sqlite3_get_table().

to_generate.name: malloc
to_generate.doc: Allocates at least size bytes of memory. Returns a pointer to the allocated memory, or 0 if size <= 0 or allocation fails. Memory must be freed with sqlite3_free().

to_generate.name: malloc64
to_generate.doc: Allocates at least size bytes of memory using 64-bit size. Returns a pointer to the allocated memory as an int64_t, or 0 if size == 0 or allocation fails. Memory must be freed with sqlite3_free().

to_generate.name: realloc
to_generate.doc: Resizes a prior memory allocation to at least size bytes. If ptr is 0, behaves like sqlite3_malloc(size). If size <= 0, behaves like sqlite3_free(ptr). Returns 0 on failure or if size <= 0.

to_generate.name: realloc64
to_generate.doc: Resizes a prior memory allocation to at least size bytes using 64-bit size. If ptr is 0, behaves like sqlite3_malloc64(size). If size is 0, behaves like sqlite3_free(ptr). Returns 0 on failure or if size == 0.

to_generate.name: free_ptr
to_generate.doc: Frees memory allocated by SQLite's memory allocation subsystem (e.g., sqlite3_malloc, sqlite3_realloc). Passing NULL is safe and results in a no-op. Only pointers obtained from SQLite's allocation functions should be passed.

to_generate.name: msize
to_generate.doc: Returns the size (in bytes) of a memory block previously allocated by SQLite's memory subsystem. Returns 0 if ptr is NULL. Only pointers obtained from SQLite's allocation functions should be passed.

to_generate.name: memory_used
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of bytes of memory currently outstanding (malloced but not freed) by SQLite's memory subsystem.

to_generate.name: memory_highwater
to_generate.doc: Returns the maximum value of memory used since the high-water mark was last reset. If reset is true, resets the high-water mark to the current memory usage and returns the previous high-water mark.

to_generate.name: randomness
to_generate.body:
void *buf = buffer_ptr != 0 ? reinterpret_cast<void*>(buffer_ptr) : nullptr;
sqlite3_randomness(length, buf);
to_generate.doc: Fills a buffer with pseudo-random bytes using SQLite's PRNG. The buffer pointer can be NULL, in which case only seeding occurs. The length parameter specifies the number of bytes to generate.

to_generate.name: set_authorizer
to_generate.doc: Registers an authorizer callback for SQL statement compilation. The callback receives action code and four optional string arguments. Return SQLITE_OK to allow, SQLITE_IGNORE to skip the action but continue compilation, or SQLITE_DENY to reject the statement.

to_generate.name: deny_code
to_generate.doc: Authorizer return code to abort the SQL statement with an error.

to_generate.name: ignore_code
to_generate.doc: Authorizer return code to deny access but not generate an error.

to_generate.name: recursive_action

to_generate.name: trace
to_generate.doc: DEPRECATED: Registers a trace callback for SQL statement execution. The callback receives the SQL statement text as a String. Use sqlite3_trace_v2() instead for new code.

to_generate.name: profile
to_generate.doc: DEPRECATED: Registers a profile callback for SQL statement execution timing. The callback receives the SQL statement text as a String and the elapsed time in nanoseconds (with millisecond resolution in current implementation). Use sqlite3_trace_v2() instead for new code.

to_generate.name: trace_stmt_mask
to_generate.doc: Trace event code for statement execution (SQL text or trigger invocation).

to_generate.name: trace_profile_mask
to_generate.doc: Trace event code for statement profiling (execution time).

to_generate.name: trace_row_mask
to_generate.doc: Trace event code for row results (each row of output).

to_generate.name: trace_close_mask
to_generate.doc: Trace event code for database connection closure.

to_generate.name: trace_v2
to_generate.doc: Registers a trace callback for SQL statement events. The callback receives: event type (int), context (int64_t), and two int64_t arguments (P and X) whose meanings depend on the event type (see SQLITE_TRACE_STMT, etc.). Returns 0 on success. The callback should return 0 for future compatibility.

to_generate.name: progress_handler
to_generate.doc: Sets a progress handler callback that is invoked periodically during long-running operations. The callback receives no arguments. If it returns non-zero, the operation is interrupted. The `n` parameter specifies the number of virtual machine instructions between callbacks; if less than 1, the handler is disabled.

to_generate.name: open
to_generate.doc: Opens an SQLite database connection. The `filename` can be a file path, ":memory:" for in-memory database, or empty string for temporary on-disk database. On success, `out_db` contains a valid handle and returns SQLITE_OK; otherwise, returns an error code and `out_db` remains invalid.

to_generate.name: open16
to_generate.doc: Opens an SQLite database connection using a UTF-16 encoded filename. Use `open()` for UTF-8 filenames unless UTF-16 is specifically required.

to_generate.name: open_v2
to_generate.doc: Opens an SQLite database connection with additional control via flags and VFS. The `flags` parameter specifies access mode and options (e.g., SQLITE_OPEN_READONLY). The `vfs` parameter specifies the VFS module name (empty string uses default).

to_generate.name: uri_parameter
to_generate.doc: Returns the value of a URI query parameter from a database filename. If the parameter is not found, returns an empty String. If the parameter exists but has no value, returns an empty String (not null). The filename must be a valid SQLite filename (e.g., obtained from SQLite core).

to_generate.name: uri_boolean
to_generate.doc: Returns the boolean interpretation of a URI query parameter. Returns true if the parameter value is 'yes', 'true', 'on' (case-insensitive) or starts with a non-zero digit; false if 'no', 'false', 'off' or starts with '0'; otherwise returns default_value. If the parameter is missing, returns default_value.

to_generate.name: uri_int64
to_generate.doc: Returns the 64-bit integer value of a URI query parameter. If the parameter is missing or not a valid integer, returns default_value.

to_generate.name: uri_key
to_generate.doc: Returns the name (key) of the N-th URI query parameter (zero-based index). Returns an empty String if the index is out of range (negative or beyond the number of parameters).

to_generate.name: filename_database
to_generate.doc: Returns the database filename corresponding to a given SQLite filename (database, journal, or WAL). The input must be a filename obtained from SQLite core (e.g., passed to VFS xOpen or from sqlite3_db_filename()). Behavior is undefined for invalid inputs.

to_generate.name: filename_journal
to_generate.doc: Returns the rollback journal filename corresponding to a given SQLite filename (database, journal, or WAL). The input must be a filename obtained from SQLite core (e.g., passed to VFS xOpen or from sqlite3_db_filename()). Behavior is undefined for invalid inputs.

to_generate.name: filename_wal
to_generate.doc: Returns the WAL (Write-Ahead Log) filename corresponding to a given SQLite filename (database, journal, or WAL). The input must be a filename obtained from SQLite core (e.g., passed to VFS xOpen or from sqlite3_db_filename()). Behavior is undefined for invalid inputs.

to_generate.name: database_file_object
to_generate.doc: Returns the sqlite3_file* handle for the main database file corresponding to a journal/WAL filename. This is intended for use in custom VFS implementations only. The input must be a filename passed to xOpen with SQLITE_OPEN_MAIN_JOURNAL or SQLITE_OPEN_WAL flags. Behavior is undefined for invalid inputs.

to_generate.name: create_filename
to_generate.doc: ⚠️ VFS-only function. Creates a filename object from database, journal, WAL, and URI parameters. The returned pointer must be freed with sqlite3_free_filename(). The input strings must remain valid until then. In Godot, ensure strings are not temporary (e.g., use static strings or manage lifetime carefully).

to_generate.name: free_filename
to_generate.doc: Frees memory allocated by sqlite3_create_filename. Must only be called on pointers returned by create_filename. Passing NULL or invalid pointers causes undefined behavior.

to_generate.name: errcode
to_generate.doc: Returns the numeric result code for the most recent API call on the database connection. Returns SQLITE_OK if no error occurred. The error code may change with subsequent API calls.

to_generate.name: extended_errcode
to_generate.doc: Returns the extended result code for the most recent API call on the database connection. Unlike errcode(), this always returns the extended code, even if extended codes are disabled globally.

to_generate.name: errmsg
to_generate.doc: Returns the English-language error message (UTF-8) for the most recent error on the database connection. Returns an empty String if no error message is available. The message is managed internally and may be overwritten by subsequent API calls.

to_generate.name: errmsg16
to_generate.doc: Returns the English-language error message (UTF-16 in native byte order) for the most recent error on the database connection. Returns an empty String if no error message is available. The message is managed internally and may be overwritten by subsequent API calls.

to_generate.name: errstr
to_generate.body:
const char* result = sqlite3_errstr(result_code);
return result ? String::utf8(result) : String();
to_generate.doc: Returns the English-language error message (UTF-8) for a given SQLite result code. Returns an empty String if the result code is not recognized.

to_generate.name: error_offset
to_generate.doc: Returns the byte offset (in UTF-8) of the start of the token that caused the most recent error. Returns -1 if the error does not reference a specific token (e.g., memory allocation failure).

to_generate.name: set_errmsg
to_generate.doc: Sets the error code and message for the database handle. If errmsg is empty, the default message for errcode is used. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, SQLITE_NOMEM on OOM, or SQLITE_MISUSE for invalid handle.
A wrapper class for an SQLite prepared statement handle.

to_generate.name: limit
to_generate.doc: Sets or queries a run-time limit for the database connection. If new_value is negative, the limit is unchanged (query mode). Returns the prior limit value. Attempts to exceed compile-time hard upper bounds are silently truncated.
A wrapper class for an SQLite prepared statement handle.

to_generate.name: limit_length
to_generate.doc: Maximum size of any string or BLOB or table row, in bytes.

to_generate.name: limit_sql_length
to_generate.doc: Maximum length of an SQL statement, in bytes.

to_generate.name: limit_column
to_generate.doc: Maximum number of columns in a table definition or in the result set of a SELECT or in an index or in an ORDER BY or GROUP BY clause.

to_generate.name: limit_expr_depth
to_generate.doc: Maximum depth of the parse tree on any expression.

to_generate.name: limit_compound_select
to_generate.doc: Maximum number of terms in a compound SELECT statement.

to_generate.name: limit_vdbe_op
to_generate.doc: Maximum number of instructions in a virtual machine program used to implement an SQL statement.

to_generate.name: limit_function_arg
to_generate.doc: Maximum number of arguments on a function.

to_generate.name: limit_attached
to_generate.doc: Maximum number of attached databases.

to_generate.name: limit_like_pattern_length
to_generate.doc: Maximum length of the pattern argument to the LIKE or GLOB operators.

to_generate.name: limit_variable_number
to_generate.doc: Maximum index number of any parameter in an SQL statement.

to_generate.name: limit_trigger_depth
to_generate.doc: Maximum depth of recursion for triggers.

to_generate.name: limit_worker_threads
to_generate.doc: Maximum number of auxiliary worker threads that a single prepared statement may start.

to_generate.name: limit_parser_depth
to_generate.doc: Maximum depth of the LALR(1) parser stack used to analyze input SQL statements.

to_generate.name: prepare_persistent_flag
to_generate.doc: Hint to the query planner that the prepared statement will be retained for a long time and probably reused many times.

to_generate.name: prepare_normalize_flag
to_generate.doc: No-op flag. Previously required for using sqlite3_normalized_sql(), but now available to all prepared statements.

to_generate.name: prepare_no_vtab_flag
to_generate.doc: Causes the SQL compiler to return an error if the statement uses any virtual tables.

to_generate.name: prepare_dont_log_flag
to_generate.doc: Prevents SQL compiler errors from being sent to the error log. Useful for test compiles without generating error messages.

to_generate.name: prepare_from_ddl_flag
to_generate.doc: Causes the SQL compiler to enforce security constraints as if the SQL statement came from an attacker. Use when preparing SQL derived from database schema.

to_generate.name: exec
to_generate.doc: Executes zero or more semicolon-separated SQL statements. If callback is provided, it is invoked for each result row with (PackedStringArray values, PackedStringArray column_names). Returning non-zero from the callback stops execution. Use get_last_error() to retrieve the error message if the return value is not SQLITE_OK.

to_generate.name: prepare
to_generate.doc: Compiles the first SQL statement from the input string into a prepared statement. Use sqlite3_prepare_v2() for new code as it's recommended over this legacy interface. Returns SQLITE_OK on success with out_stmt containing the prepared statement handle. out_tail receives the unused portion of the SQL string.

to_generate.name: prepare_v2
to_generate.doc: Compiles the first SQL statement from the input string into a prepared statement using the recommended sqlite3_prepare_v2() interface. Returns SQLITE_OK on success with out_stmt containing the prepared statement handle. out_tail receives the unused portion of the SQL string. This version automatically recompiles on schema changes and returns detailed error codes.

to_generate.name: prepare_v3
to_generate.doc: Compiles the first SQL statement from the input string into a prepared statement using sqlite3_prepare_v3() with custom prepFlags. Returns SQLITE_OK on success with out_stmt containing the prepared statement handle. out_tail receives the unused portion of the SQL string.

to_generate.name: prepare16
to_generate.doc: Compiles the first SQL statement from the input string into a prepared statement using sqlite3_prepare16() (UTF-16 version). Returns SQLITE_OK on success with out_stmt containing the prepared statement handle. out_tail receives the unused portion of the SQL string. Note: The UTF-8 versions (prepare, prepare_v2, prepare_v3) are recommended for new code.

to_generate.name: prepare16_v2
to_generate.doc: Compiles the first SQL statement from the input string into a prepared statement using sqlite3_prepare16_v2() (UTF-16 version). Returns SQLITE_OK on success with out_stmt containing the prepared statement handle. out_tail receives the unused portion of the SQL string. This version automatically recompiles on schema changes and returns detailed error codes. Note: The UTF-8 versions (prepare, prepare_v2, prepare_v3) are recommended for new code.

to_generate.name: prepare16_v3
to_generate.doc: Compiles the first SQL statement from the input string into a prepared statement using sqlite3_prepare16_v3() (UTF-16 version with custom prepFlags). Returns SQLITE_OK on success with out_stmt containing the prepared statement handle. out_tail receives the unused portion of the SQL string. Note: The UTF-8 versions (prepare, prepare_v2, prepare_v3) are recommended for new code.

to_generate.name: get_sql
to_generate.doc: Returns the UTF-8 SQL text used to create the prepared statement. The returned string is managed by SQLite and is automatically freed when the prepared statement is finalized.

to_generate.name: get_expanded_sql
to_generate.doc: Returns the UTF-8 SQL text of the prepared statement with bound parameters expanded. The returned string is allocated by SQLite and must be freed by the application. For example, if a parameter is bound to 2345, it will show "2345" instead of "$abc".

to_generate.name: get_normalized_sql
to_generate.doc: Returns the UTF-8 normalized SQL text of the prepared statement. The semantics used to normalize a SQL statement are unspecified and subject to change. At a minimum, literal values will be replaced with suitable placeholders. This function is only available if SQLITE_ENABLE_NORMALIZE is defined.

to_generate.name: is_read_only
to_generate.doc: Returns true if the prepared statement makes no direct changes to the database file. Note that application-defined SQL functions or virtual tables might change the database indirectly as a side effect.

to_generate.name: is_explain
to_generate.doc: Returns 1 if the prepared statement is an EXPLAIN statement, 2 if it is an EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN statement, or 0 if it is an ordinary statement or NULL.

to_generate.name: set_explain_mode
to_generate.doc: Changes the EXPLAIN setting for the prepared statement. If mode is 0, the statement becomes normal. If mode is 1, it behaves as if its SQL text began with 'EXPLAIN'. If mode is 2, it behaves as if its SQL text began with 'EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN'. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: is_busy
to_generate.doc: Returns true if the prepared statement has been stepped at least once using sqlite3_step() but has neither run to completion (returned SQLITE_DONE) nor been reset using sqlite3_reset(). Returns false if the statement is not busy or is NULL.
A wrapper class for SQLite's dynamically typed value object. Represents all values that can be stored in a database table: integers, floating point values, strings, BLOBs, or NULL.
A wrapper class for SQLite's SQL function context object. This is the first parameter to application-defined SQL functions and contains the execution context.

to_generate.name: bind_blob
to_generate.doc: Binds a BLOB value to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise. If data is empty, binds NULL instead. The data is copied and managed by SQLite (SQLITE_TRANSIENT).

to_generate.name: bind_blob64
to_generate.doc: Binds a BLOB value to a parameter in the prepared statement using 64-bit size. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise. If data is empty, binds NULL instead. The data is copied and managed by SQLite (SQLITE_TRANSIENT).

to_generate.name: bind_double
to_generate.doc: Binds a double-precision floating-point value to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: bind_int
to_generate.doc: Binds a 32-bit integer value to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: bind_int64
to_generate.doc: Binds a 64-bit integer value to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: bind_null
to_generate.doc: Binds a NULL value to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: bind_text
to_generate.doc: Binds a UTF-8 text string to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise. If text is empty, binds NULL instead. The string is copied and managed by SQLite (SQLITE_TRANSIENT).

to_generate.name: bind_text16
to_generate.doc: Binds a UTF-16 text string to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise. If text is empty, binds NULL instead. The string is copied and managed by SQLite (SQLITE_TRANSIENT).

to_generate.name: bind_text64
to_generate.doc: Binds a UTF-8 text string to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise. If text is empty, binds NULL instead. The string is copied and managed by SQLite (SQLITE_TRANSIENT).
A wrapper class for SQLite value handles.

to_generate.name: bind_value
to_generate.doc: Binds a value from an sqlite3_value object to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: bind_pointer
to_generate.doc: Binds a pointer value to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise. The pointer is stored as an int64_t for compatibility with Godot's binding system.

to_generate.name: bind_zeroblob
to_generate.doc: Binds a zero-filled BLOB of specified length to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise. A negative length results in a zero-length BLOB.

to_generate.name: bind_zeroblob64
to_generate.doc: Binds a zero-filled BLOB of specified 64-bit length to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: bind_parameter_count
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of SQL parameters in the prepared statement. This is the index of the largest (rightmost) parameter, which corresponds to the number of unique parameters for most parameter forms.

to_generate.name: bind_parameter_name
to_generate.doc: Returns the name of the N-th SQL parameter in the prepared statement. Returns an empty String if the parameter has no name or if the index is out of range.

to_generate.name: bind_parameter_index
to_generate.doc: Returns the index of an SQL parameter given its name. Returns 0 if no matching parameter is found.

to_generate.name: clear_bindings
to_generate.doc: Resets all host parameters on the prepared statement to NULL. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: column_count
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of columns in the result set returned by the prepared statement. Returns 0 if the statement returns no data (e.g., an UPDATE statement).

to_generate.name: column_name
to_generate.doc: Returns the name assigned to a particular column in the result set. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the name is NULL.

to_generate.name: column_name16
to_generate.doc: Returns the name assigned to a particular column in the result set as a UTF-16 string. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the name is NULL.

to_generate.name: column_database_name
to_generate.doc: Returns the database name that is the origin of a particular result column. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the name is NULL.

to_generate.name: column_database_name16
to_generate.doc: Returns the database name that is the origin of a particular result column as a UTF-16 string. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the name is NULL.

to_generate.name: column_table_name
to_generate.doc: Returns the table name that is the origin of a particular result column. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the name is NULL.

to_generate.name: column_table_name16
to_generate.doc: Returns the table name that is the origin of a particular result column as a UTF-16 string. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the name is NULL.

to_generate.name: column_origin_name
to_generate.doc: Returns the column name that is the origin of a particular result column. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the name is NULL.

to_generate.name: column_origin_name16
to_generate.doc: Returns the column name that is the origin of a particular result column as a UTF-16 string. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the name is NULL.

to_generate.name: column_decltype
to_generate.doc: Returns the declared datatype of a query result column. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the column is an expression or subquery.

to_generate.name: column_decltype16
to_generate.doc: Returns the declared datatype of a query result column as a UTF-16 string. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the column is an expression or subquery.

to_generate.name: step
to_generate.doc: Evaluates a prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise. For SELECT statements, SQLITE_ROW is returned when a new row of data is ready, and SQLITE_DONE is returned when the statement has finished executing.

to_generate.name: data_count
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of columns in the current row of the result set. Returns 0 if the prepared statement does not have results ready or if the previous call to step() returned SQLITE_DONE.

to_generate.name: constant_integer
to_generate.doc: 64-bit signed integer type code.

to_generate.name: constant_float
to_generate.doc: 64-bit IEEE floating point number type code.

to_generate.name: constant_blob
to_generate.doc: BLOB type code.

to_generate.name: constant_null
to_generate.doc: NULL type code.

to_generate.name: constant_text
to_generate.doc: String type code.

to_generate.name: constant_text
to_generate.doc: String type code (SQLite 3 version).

to_generate.name: column_blob
to_generate.doc: Returns a BLOB result from a query as a PackedByteArray. Returns an empty PackedByteArray if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

to_generate.name: column_double
to_generate.doc: Returns a REAL (floating-point) result from a query. Returns 0.0 if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

to_generate.name: column_int
to_generate.doc: Returns a 32-bit INTEGER result from a query. Returns 0 if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

to_generate.name: column_int64
to_generate.doc: Returns a 64-bit INTEGER result from a query. Returns 0 if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

to_generate.name: column_text
to_generate.doc: Returns a UTF-8 TEXT result from a query as a String. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

to_generate.name: column_text16
to_generate.doc: Returns a UTF-16 TEXT result from a query as a String. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

to_generate.name: column_value
to_generate.doc: Returns the result column as an sqlite3_value object. Note: The returned value is an unprotected sqlite3_value object and should only be used safely with bind_value() and result_value() in multithreaded environments. Returns an empty Ref if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

to_generate.name: column_bytes
to_generate.doc: Returns the size in bytes of a BLOB or UTF-8 TEXT result. Returns 0 if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

to_generate.name: column_bytes16
to_generate.doc: Returns the size in bytes of a BLOB or UTF-16 TEXT result. Returns 0 if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

to_generate.name: column_type
to_generate.doc: Returns the default datatype code of the result column (SQLITE_INTEGER, SQLITE_FLOAT, SQLITE_TEXT, SQLITE_BLOB, or SQLITE_NULL). Returns SQLITE_NULL if the column index is out of range.

to_generate.name: finalize
to_generate.doc: Destroys a prepared statement object. Returns SQLITE_OK if successful, or an error code if the most recent evaluation encountered errors. Calling with a null statement is a no-op.

to_generate.name: reset
to_generate.doc: Resets a prepared statement object back to its initial state, ready to be re-executed. Returns SQLITE_OK if successful, or an error code if the most recent evaluation encountered errors. Bound variables retain their values.

to_generate.name: create_function
to_generate.doc: Creates or redefines a SQL function. Note: This is a simplified wrapper that may not handle all callback scenarios properly. For full functionality, proper handling of sqlite3_context and sqlite3_value is required.

to_generate.name: create_function16
to_generate.doc: Creates or redefines a SQL function with UTF-16 function name. Note: This is a simplified wrapper that may not handle all callback scenarios properly.

to_generate.name: create_function_v2
to_generate.doc: Creates or redefines a SQL function with destructor callback. Note: This is a simplified wrapper that may not handle all callback scenarios properly.

to_generate.name: create_window_function
to_generate.doc: Creates or redefines a SQL window function. Note: This is a simplified wrapper that may not handle all callback scenarios properly.

to_generate.name: constant_utf8
to_generate.doc: Text is encoded as UTF-8.

to_generate.name: constant_utf16le
to_generate.doc: Text is encoded as UTF-16 with little endian byte order.

to_generate.name: constant_utf16be
to_generate.doc: Text is encoded as UTF-16 with big endian byte order.

to_generate.name: constant_utf16
to_generate.doc: Text is encoded as UTF-16 with native byte order.

to_generate.name: constant_any
to_generate.doc: Deprecated encoding value. Do not use in new applications.

to_generate.name: constant_utf16_aligned
to_generate.doc: Text is encoded as UTF-16 with native byte order and 2-byte alignment.

to_generate.name: constant_utf8_zt
to_generate.doc: Zero-terminated UTF-8 encoding.

to_generate.name: constant_deterministic
to_generate.doc: Function is deterministic (same output for same inputs).

to_generate.name: constant_directonly
to_generate.doc: Function may only be invoked from top-level SQL.

to_generate.name: constant_subtype
to_generate.doc: Function may inspect argument subtypes.

to_generate.name: constant_innocuous
to_generate.doc: Function is unlikely to cause problems even if misused.

to_generate.name: constant_result_subtype
to_generate.doc: Function may set result subtype.

to_generate.name: constant_selforder1
to_generate.doc: Function is an aggregate that internally orders values.

to_generate.name: aggregate_count
to_generate.doc: Deprecated: Returns the number of values passed to the aggregate function.

to_generate.name: expired
to_generate.doc: Deprecated: Returns non-zero if the prepared statement has been invalidated.

to_generate.name: value_blob
to_generate.doc: Extracts a BLOB value from a protected sqlite3_value object as a PackedByteArray. Returns an empty PackedByteArray if the value is NULL or if the value handle is invalid.

to_generate.name: value_double
to_generate.doc: Extracts a REAL (floating-point) value from a protected sqlite3_value object. Returns 0.0 if the value handle is invalid.

to_generate.name: value_int
to_generate.doc: Extracts a 32-bit INTEGER value from a protected sqlite3_value object. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid.

to_generate.name: value_int64
to_generate.doc: Extracts a 64-bit INTEGER value from a protected sqlite3_value object. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid.

to_generate.name: value_pointer
to_generate.doc: Extracts a pointer value from a protected sqlite3_value object. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid or if the pointer type doesn't match.

to_generate.name: value_text
to_generate.doc: Extracts a UTF-8 TEXT value from a protected sqlite3_value object as a String. Returns an empty String if the value handle is invalid or if the value is NULL.

to_generate.name: value_text16
to_generate.doc: Extracts a UTF-16 TEXT value from a protected sqlite3_value object as a String. Returns an empty String if the value handle is invalid or if the value is NULL.

to_generate.name: value_text16le
to_generate.doc: Extracts a UTF-16 little-endian TEXT value from a protected sqlite3_value object as a String. Returns an empty String if the value handle is invalid or if the value is NULL.

to_generate.name: value_text16be
to_generate.doc: Extracts a UTF-16 big-endian TEXT value from a protected sqlite3_value object as a String. Returns an empty String if the value handle is invalid or if the value is NULL.

to_generate.name: value_bytes
to_generate.doc: Returns the size in bytes of a BLOB or UTF-8 TEXT value from a protected sqlite3_value object. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid.

to_generate.name: value_bytes16
to_generate.doc: Returns the size in bytes of a BLOB or UTF-16 TEXT value from a protected sqlite3_value object. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid.

to_generate.name: value_type
to_generate.doc: Returns the default datatype code of the value from a protected sqlite3_value object (SQLITE_INTEGER, SQLITE_FLOAT, SQLITE_TEXT, SQLITE_BLOB, or SQLITE_NULL). Returns SQLITE_NULL if the value handle is invalid.

to_generate.name: value_numeric_type
to_generate.doc: Returns the numeric datatype code of the value from a protected sqlite3_value object (SQLITE_INTEGER, SQLITE_FLOAT, or SQLITE_NULL). Returns SQLITE_NULL if the value handle is invalid.

to_generate.name: value_nochange
to_generate.doc: Returns 1 if the value is part of a row that is being updated in a UPDATE statement and the value has not changed, and 0 otherwise. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid.

to_generate.name: value_frombind
to_generate.doc: Returns 1 if the value originated from a bound parameter in an SQL statement, and 0 otherwise. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid.

to_generate.name: value_encoding
to_generate.doc: Returns the internal text encoding state of an sqlite3_value object (SQLITE_UTF8, SQLITE_UTF16BE, or SQLITE_UTF16LE) if the value is of TEXT type. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid.

to_generate.name: value_subtype
to_generate.doc: Returns the subtype for an application-defined SQL function argument. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid.

to_generate.name: value_dup
to_generate.doc: Makes a copy of the sqlite3_value object V and returns a pointer to that copy. Returns NULL if V is NULL or if a memory allocation fails. If V is a pointer value, then the result is a NULL value.

to_generate.name: value_free
to_generate.doc: Frees an sqlite3_value object previously obtained from sqlite3_value_dup(). If value is NULL then this function is a harmless no-op.

to_generate.name: value_free
to_generate.doc: Frees an sqlite3_value object previously obtained from sqlite3_value_dup(). If value is NULL then this function is a harmless no-op.

to_generate.name: aggregate_context
to_generate.doc: Allocates memory for storing aggregate function state. On first call, allocates N bytes, zeroes them, and returns pointer. On subsequent calls for the same aggregate function instance, returns the same buffer. Returns 0 if context is invalid or nBytes <= 0.

to_generate.name: user_data
to_generate.doc: Returns the user data pointer that was passed when the function was registered. Returns 0 if the context is invalid.

to_generate.name: context_db_handle
to_generate.doc: Returns the database connection handle associated with the function context. Returns NULL if the context is invalid.

to_generate.name: get_auxdata
to_generate.doc: Returns the auxiliary data associated with the Nth argument of the application-defined function. Returns 0 if there is no auxiliary data or if the context is invalid.

to_generate.name: set_auxdata
to_generate.doc: Sets auxiliary data for the Nth argument of the application-defined function. Uses NULL as the destructor (no cleanup).

to_generate.name: get_clientdata
to_generate.doc: Returns the client data pointer associated with the database connection and name. Returns 0 if there is no associated data or if the database handle is invalid.

to_generate.name: set_clientdata
to_generate.doc: Sets client data associated with the database connection and name. Uses NULL as the destructor (no cleanup). Returns 0 on success, SQLITE_NOMEM on allocation failure.

to_generate.name: constant_sqlite_static
to_generate.doc: Special destructor value indicating the content pointer is constant and will never change. No destruction needed.

to_generate.name: constant_sqlite_transient
to_generate.doc: Special destructor value indicating the content will likely change and SQLite should make its own private copy.

to_generate.name: result_blob
to_generate.doc: Sets the result from an application-defined function to be the BLOB specified by the value. Uses SQLITE_TRANSIENT as the destructor (data is copied).

to_generate.name: result_blob64
to_generate.doc: Sets the result from an application-defined function to be the BLOB specified by the value. Uses SQLITE_TRANSIENT as the destructor (data is copied).

to_generate.name: result_double
to_generate.doc: Sets the result from an application-defined function to be the floating point value specified.

to_generate.name: result_error
to_generate.doc: Causes the implemented SQL function to throw an error with the specified message. Uses -1 for the length parameter to let SQLite search for the null terminator.

to_generate.name: result_error16
to_generate.doc: Causes the implemented SQL function to throw an error with the specified UTF-16 message. Uses -1 for the length parameter to let SQLite search for the null terminator.

to_generate.name: result_error_toobig
to_generate.doc: Causes SQLite to throw an error indicating that a string or BLOB is too long to represent.

to_generate.name: result_error_nomem
to_generate.doc: Causes SQLite to throw an error indicating that a memory allocation failed.

to_generate.name: result_error_code
to_generate.doc: Changes the error code returned by SQLite as a result of an error in a function.

to_generate.name: result_int
to_generate.doc: Sets the return value of an application-defined function to be the 32-bit signed integer value specified.

to_generate.name: result_int64
to_generate.doc: Sets the return value of an application-defined function to be the 64-bit signed integer value specified.

to_generate.name: result_null
to_generate.doc: Sets the return value of an application-defined function to be NULL.

to_generate.name: result_text
to_generate.doc: Sets the return value of an application-defined function to be the UTF-8 text string specified. Uses SQLITE_TRANSIENT as the destructor (data is copied).

to_generate.name: result_text64
to_generate.doc: Sets the return value of an application-defined function to be the text string specified in the given encoding. Uses SQLITE_TRANSIENT as the destructor (data is copied).

to_generate.name: result_text16
to_generate.doc: Sets the return value of an application-defined function to be the UTF-16 text string specified. Uses SQLITE_TRANSIENT as the destructor (data is copied).

to_generate.name: result_text16le
to_generate.doc: Sets the return value of an application-defined function to be the UTF-16 little endian text string specified. Uses SQLITE_TRANSIENT as the destructor (data is copied).

to_generate.name: result_text16be
to_generate.doc: Sets the return value of an application-defined function to be the UTF-16 big endian text string specified. Uses SQLITE_TRANSIENT as the destructor (data is copied). Note: The input string should be in big endian format if the system's native byte order is not big endian.

to_generate.name: result_value
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of an application-defined function to be a copy of the sqlite3_value object. The input value can be protected or unprotected.

to_generate.name: result_pointer
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of an application-defined function to be a pointer. The destructor parameter is omitted (uses nullptr).

to_generate.name: result_zeroblob
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of an application-defined function to be a BLOB of zeros containing n bytes.

to_generate.name: result_zeroblob64
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of an application-defined function to be a BLOB of zeros containing n bytes. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code on failure.

to_generate.name: result_subtype
to_generate.doc: Sets the subtype of the result from an application-defined SQL function. Only the lower 8 bits of the subtype are preserved in current versions of SQLite. The function should be used with the SQLITE_RESULT_SUBTYPE property when registering the SQL function.

to_generate.name: create_collation
to_generate.doc: Adds a new collation to the database connection. The compare_func callback receives two String arguments and must return an integer that is negative, zero, or positive if the first string is less than, equal to, or greater than the second, respectively.

to_generate.name: create_collation_v2
to_generate.doc: Adds a new collation to the database connection with the ability to specify a destructor callback. The destructor parameter is omitted (uses nullptr). The compare_func callback receives two String arguments and must return an integer that is negative, zero, or positive if the first string is less than, equal to, or greater than the second, respectively.

to_generate.name: create_collation16
to_generate.doc: Adds a new collation to the database connection with a UTF-16 name. The compare_func callback receives two String arguments and must return an integer that is negative, zero, or positive if the first string is less than, equal to, or greater than the second, respectively.

to_generate.name: collation_needed
to_generate.doc: Registers a callback to be invoked whenever an undefined collation sequence is required. The callback receives three arguments: a Ref<Sqlite3Handle> for the database connection, an integer for the text representation, and a String for the collation name.

to_generate.name: collation_needed16
to_generate.doc: Registers a callback to be invoked whenever an undefined collation sequence is required. The callback receives three arguments: a Ref<Sqlite3Handle> for the database connection, an integer for the text representation, and a String for the collation name (UTF-16 encoded).

to_generate.name: activate_cerod
to_generate.doc: Activates the CEROD (Cryptography Enabled Read-Only Database) functionality with the specified passphrase. This function is only available when SQLite is compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_CEROD.

to_generate.name: sleep
to_generate.doc: Suspends execution for at least the specified number of milliseconds. Returns the actual number of milliseconds slept. If a negative argument is passed, it is changed to zero.

to_generate.name: set_temp_directory
to_generate.doc: Sets the temporary directory path. The path is converted to UTF-8 and stored using sqlite3_mprintf. Note: This function should be called before opening any database connections and only from a single thread.

to_generate.name: get_temp_directory
to_generate.doc: Gets the current temporary directory path. Returns an empty String if the temporary directory is not set.

to_generate.name: set_data_directory
to_generate.doc: Sets the data directory path for database files. Only used by the Windows VFS. The path is converted to UTF-8 and stored using sqlite3_mprintf. Note: This function should be called before opening any database connections and only from a single thread.

to_generate.name: get_data_directory
to_generate.doc: Gets the current data directory path. Returns an empty String if the data directory is not set. Only used by the Windows VFS.

to_generate.name: win32_set_directory
to_generate.doc: Sets the value associated with sqlite3_temp_directory or sqlite3_data_directory. Pass an empty String to free the previous value. This function is Windows-specific.

to_generate.name: win32_set_directory8
to_generate.doc: Sets the value associated with sqlite3_temp_directory or sqlite3_data_directory using a UTF-8 string. Pass an empty String to free the previous value. This function is Windows-specific.

to_generate.name: win32_set_directory16
to_generate.body:
const void *value_ptr = value.length() > 0 ? (const void*)value.utf16().ptr() : nullptr;
return sqlite3_win32_set_directory16(type, value_ptr);
to_generate.doc: Sets the value associated with sqlite3_temp_directory or sqlite3_data_directory using a UTF-16 string. Pass an empty String to free the previous value. This function is Windows-specific.

to_generate.name: constant_win32_data_directory_type
to_generate.doc: Constant for the data directory type in sqlite3_win32_set_directory.

to_generate.name: constant_win32_temp_directory_type
to_generate.doc: Constant for the temporary directory type in sqlite3_win32_set_directory.

to_generate.name: get_autocommit
to_generate.doc: Returns true if the database connection is in autocommit mode, false otherwise. Autocommit mode is on by default and is disabled by BEGIN statements.

to_generate.name: db_handle
to_generate.doc: Returns the database connection handle to which the prepared statement belongs.

to_generate.name: db_name
to_generate.doc: Returns the schema name for the N-th database on the database connection. Returns an empty String if N is out of range. Note: The returned string is managed by SQLite and may be deallocated by schema changes.

to_generate.name: db_filename
to_generate.doc: Returns the absolute pathname of the filename associated with the specified database. Returns an empty String if the database doesn't exist or is temporary/in-memory.

to_generate.name: db_readonly
to_generate.body:
ERR_FAIL_COND_V(db.is_null() || !db->is_valid(), -1);
return sqlite3_db_readonly(db->handle, db_name.length() > 0 ? db_name.utf8().get_data() : nullptr);
to_generate.doc: Returns 1 if the database is read-only, 0 if it is read/write, or -1 if the database name is not valid.

to_generate.name: txn_state
to_generate.body:
ERR_FAIL_COND_V(db.is_null() || !db->is_valid(), -1);
return sqlite3_txn_state(db->handle, schema.length() > 0 ? schema.utf8().get_data() : nullptr);
to_generate.doc: Returns the transaction state of the schema: 0 (SQLITE_TXN_NONE) for no transaction, 1 (SQLITE_TXN_READ) for read transaction, 2 (SQLITE_TXN_WRITE) for write transaction, or -1 if the schema name is not valid.

to_generate.name: constant_txn_none
to_generate.doc: Constant indicating no transaction is currently pending.

to_generate.name: constant_txn_read
to_generate.doc: Constant indicating the database is currently in a read transaction.

to_generate.name: constant_txn_write
to_generate.doc: Constant indicating the database is currently in a write transaction.

to_generate.name: next_stmt
to_generate.doc: Returns the next prepared statement after the given statement. If stmt is null, returns the first prepared statement. Returns an empty Ref if no more statements are available.

to_generate.name: commit_hook
to_generate.doc: Registers a callback to be invoked whenever a transaction is committed. If the callback returns non-zero, the commit is converted to a rollback. Returns the previous callback's user data as uint64_t. Registering an invalid Callable disables the callback.

to_generate.name: rollback_hook
to_generate.doc: Registers a callback to be invoked whenever a transaction is rolled back. Returns the previous callback's user data as uint64_t. Registering an invalid Callable disables the callback.

to_generate.name: autovacuum_pages
to_generate.doc: Registers a callback to be invoked prior to each autovacuum of the database file. The callback receives the schema name, database file size in pages, number of free pages, and bytes per page as arguments. It should return the number of free pages to remove. Returns SQLITE_OK (0) on success or an error code on failure. Registering an invalid Callable disables the callback.

to_generate.name: update_hook
to_generate.doc: Registers a callback to be invoked when a row is updated, inserted, or deleted in a rowid table. The callback receives the operation type (SQLITE_INSERT, SQLITE_DELETE, or SQLITE_UPDATE), database name, table name, and rowid as arguments. Returns the previous callback's user data as uint64_t. Registering an invalid Callable disables the callback.

to_generate.name: enable_shared_cache
to_generate.doc: Enables or disables the sharing of the database cache and schema data structures between connections to the same database. This is a global setting that affects all subsequent calls to sqlite3_open(), sqlite3_open_v2(), and sqlite3_open16(). Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: release_memory
to_generate.doc: Attempts to free the specified number of bytes of heap memory by deallocating non-essential memory allocations such as database page caches. Returns the number of bytes actually freed, which might be more or less than the amount requested. Returns 0 if SQLite is not compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_MEMORY_MANAGEMENT.

to_generate.name: release_memory
to_generate.doc: Attempts to free as much heap memory as possible from the specified database connection. Unlike sqlite3_release_memory(), this works even when SQLITE_ENABLE_MEMORY_MANAGEMENT is omitted. Returns the number of bytes actually freed.

to_generate.name: soft_heap_limit
to_generate.doc: Sets or queries the soft limit on the amount of heap memory that may be allocated by SQLite. If limit is negative, only the current limit is returned without making changes. Returns the previous limit size, or negative in case of error. Setting the limit to zero disables the heap limiter mechanism.

to_generate.name: hard_heap_limit
to_generate.doc: Sets or queries the hard upper bound on the amount of memory that will be allocated. If limit is negative, only the current limit is returned without making changes. Returns the previous limit size, or negative in case of error. Setting the limit to zero disables the heap limiter mechanism.

to_generate.name: soft_heap_limit
to_generate.doc: Deprecated. Sets the soft limit on the amount of heap memory that may be allocated by SQLite. Use sqlite3_soft_heap_limit64() instead. Setting limit to 0 disables the heap limiter mechanism.

to_generate.name: table_column_metadata
to_generate.doc: Extracts metadata about a column of a table. Returns a Dictionary with keys: data_type, collation_sequence, not_null, primary_key, and auto_increment. Returns an empty Dictionary if the column does not exist or an error occurs.

to_generate.name: load_extension
to_generate.doc: Loads an SQLite extension library from the specified file. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or SQLITE_ERROR on failure. Extension loading must be enabled using sqlite3_enable_load_extension() or sqlite3_db_config() with SQLITE_DBCONFIG_ENABLE_LOAD_EXTENSION before calling this function.

to_generate.name: enable_load_extension
to_generate.doc: Enables or disables extension loading for the specified database connection. This enables or disables both the C-API sqlite3_load_extension() and the SQL function load_extension(). Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code otherwise. For security reasons, consider using sqlite3_db_config() with SQLITE_DBCONFIG_ENABLE_LOAD_EXTENSION to enable only the C-API.

to_generate.name: auto_extension
to_generate.doc: Registers an entry point to be automatically invoked for each new database connection. Note: This function uses a C language trick where the function pointer is declared with one signature but called with another. The full functionality is not easily translatable to higher-level languages. For now, this is a stub that returns SQLITE_OK.

to_generate.name: cancel_auto_extension
to_generate.doc: Unregisters an initialization routine that was registered using sqlite3_auto_extension. Returns 1 if the routine was successfully unregistered, 0 if it was not on the list. Note: This function uses a C language trick where the function pointer is declared with one signature but called with another. The full functionality is not easily translatable to higher-level languages. For now, this is a stub that returns 0.

to_generate.name: reset_auto_extension
to_generate.doc: Disables all automatic extensions previously registered using sqlite3_auto_extension().

to_generate.name: create_module
to_generate.doc: Registers a new virtual table module name on the database connection. The module name is registered with the provided methods and client data. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: create_module_v2
to_generate.doc: Registers a new virtual table module name with a destructor callback. The destructor is called when SQLite no longer needs the client data. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: drop_modules
to_generate.doc: Removes virtual table modules from the database connection, keeping only those named in the keep array. If keep is empty, all modules are removed. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code otherwise.
Wrapper for the sqlite3_vtab virtual table instance structure.
Wrapper for the sqlite3_vtab_cursor virtual table cursor structure.

to_generate.name: declare_vtab
to_generate.doc: Declares the schema of a virtual table. The SQL statement should be a CREATE TABLE statement that defines the column names and types.

to_generate.name: overload_function
to_generate.doc: Ensures a global version of a function exists so it can be overloaded by a virtual table. If the function doesn't exist, it's created with an implementation that throws an exception. The nArg parameter specifies the number of arguments (-1 for any number).
A wrapper class for an open BLOB handle used for incremental BLOB I/O operations.

to_generate.name: blob_open
to_generate.doc: Opens a BLOB handle for incremental I/O operations. The BLOB is located in row iRow, column zColumn, table zTable in database zDb. Parameter zDb is the symbolic name of the database (main/temp/attached). If flags is non-zero, the BLOB is opened for read/write access; if zero, read-only. On success, returns SQLITE_OK and stores the BLOB handle in out_blob. Otherwise returns an error code and sets out_blob handle to NULL.

to_generate.name: blob_reopen
to_generate.doc: Moves an existing BLOB handle to a new row of the same database table. The new row is identified by the rowid value. Only the row can be changed - the database, table and column remain the same. This is faster than closing the existing handle and opening a new one. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code if the new row doesn't exist or doesn't contain a blob/text value.

to_generate.name: blob_close
to_generate.doc: Closes an open BLOB handle. The handle is closed unconditionally even if this routine returns an error code. If the blob handle was opened for read-write access and the database is in auto-commit mode with no other active write operations, the current transaction is committed. Passing NULL or an invalid handle is a harmless no-op.

to_generate.name: blob_bytes
to_generate.doc: Returns the size in bytes of the BLOB accessible via the BLOB handle. This routine only works on successfully opened BLOB handles that have not been closed. Returns -1 if the handle is invalid.

to_generate.name: blob_read
to_generate.doc: Reads data from an open BLOB handle into a PackedByteArray. Reads 'size' bytes starting from 'offset' in the BLOB. Returns an empty PackedByteArray on error. The offset and size must be valid (offset >= 0, size >= 0, offset + size <= blob size).

to_generate.name: blob_write
to_generate.doc: Writes data into an open BLOB handle. Writes the contents of 'data' starting at 'offset' in the BLOB. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, SQLITE_READONLY if the BLOB handle was not opened for writing, or other error codes for various failure conditions. Cannot increase the size of the BLOB.
A wrapper class for a Virtual File System (VFS) object used by SQLite.

to_generate.name: vfs_find
to_generate.doc: Finds a Virtual File System (VFS) by name. Returns a handle to the VFS if found, or an empty handle if not found. Names are case-sensitive. If vfs_name is empty, returns the default VFS.

to_generate.name: vfs_register
to_generate.doc: Registers a Virtual File System (VFS). If make_default is true, this VFS becomes the default. The same VFS can be registered multiple times. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code on failure.

to_generate.name: vfs_unregister
to_generate.doc: Unregisters a Virtual File System (VFS). If the default VFS is unregistered, another VFS is chosen as default. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code on failure.
A wrapper class for a SQLite mutex handle.

to_generate.name: mutex_alloc
to_generate.doc: Allocates a new mutex. The type parameter must be one of the SQLITE_MUTEX_* constants. Dynamic mutexes (SQLITE_MUTEX_FAST and SQLITE_MUTEX_RECURSIVE) create new mutexes on each call, while static mutexes return the same mutex for the same type number.

to_generate.name: mutex_free
to_generate.doc: Deallocates a previously allocated dynamic mutex. Attempting to deallocate a static mutex results in undefined behavior. After calling this function, the mutex handle is no longer valid.

to_generate.name: mutex_enter
to_generate.doc: Enters a mutex. If another thread is already within the mutex, this function will block until the mutex is available. Mutexes created with SQLITE_MUTEX_RECURSIVE can be entered multiple times by the same thread.

to_generate.name: mutex_try
to_generate.doc: Attempts to enter a mutex without blocking. Returns SQLITE_OK upon successful entry, or SQLITE_BUSY if another thread is already within the mutex. Mutexes created with SQLITE_MUTEX_RECURSIVE can be entered multiple times by the same thread. On systems that don't support this operation, this function will always return SQLITE_BUSY.

to_generate.name: mutex_leave
to_generate.doc: Exits a mutex that was previously entered by the same thread. The behavior is undefined if the mutex is not currently entered by the calling thread or is not currently allocated.

A wrapper class for the SQLite dynamic string object handle.

to_generate.name: str_new
to_generate.doc: Creates a new dynamic string object. The returned object must be freed by a subsequent call to str_finish(). If the db parameter is NULL, the maximum length of the string will be SQLITE_MAX_LENGTH; otherwise, it will be the value set for sqlite3_limit(D, SQLITE_LIMIT_LENGTH).

to_generate.name: str_finish
to_generate.doc: Destroys the sqlite3_str object and returns the constructed string. The returned string is automatically freed after conversion to a Godot String. Returns an empty String if the input is invalid or if the string is zero bytes long.

to_generate.name: str_free
to_generate.doc: Destroys both the sqlite3_str object and the string content it contains. This is equivalent to calling sqlite3_free(sqlite3_str_finish(X)).

to_generate.name: str_appendf
to_generate.doc: Appends formatted text to the dynamic string using SQLite's printf functionality. The format string uses SQLite's printf formatting rules.

to_generate.name: str_vappendf
to_generate.doc: Appends formatted text to the dynamic string using SQLite's printf functionality with a PackedStringArray of arguments.

to_generate.name: str_append
to_generate.doc: Appends exactly N bytes from the text string onto the dynamic string. If length is negative, appends the entire string. If length is specified, appends at most that many bytes.

to_generate.name: str_appendall
to_generate.doc: Appends the complete content of the zero-terminated string onto the dynamic string.

to_generate.name: str_appendchar
to_generate.doc: Appends N copies of the single-byte character C onto the end of the dynamic string. The character is specified as an integer code point. Can be used for whitespace indentation.

to_generate.name: str_reset
to_generate.doc: Resets the string under construction inside the sqlite3_str object back to zero bytes in length.

to_generate.name: str_truncate
to_generate.doc: Changes the length of the string under construction to be N bytes or less. This routine is a no-op if N is negative or if the string is already N bytes or smaller in size.

to_generate.name: str_errcode
to_generate.doc: Returns the current error code of the sqlite3_str object. Returns SQLITE_NOMEM for out-of-memory errors, SQLITE_TOOBIG if the size exceeds SQLITE_MAX_LENGTH, or SQLITE_OK if there are no errors.

to_generate.name: str_length
to_generate.doc: Returns the current length in bytes of the dynamic string under construction. The returned length does not include the zero-termination byte.

to_generate.name: str_value
to_generate.doc: Returns a pointer to the current content of the dynamic string under construction. The returned String is a copy and is safe to use after subsequent method calls on the same object.

to_generate.name: status
to_generate.doc: Retrieves runtime status information about SQLite's performance. Returns a Dictionary with 'current' and 'highwater' keys. Returns an empty Dictionary on error.

to_generate.name: status64
to_generate.doc: Retrieves runtime status information about SQLite's performance using 64-bit integers. Returns a Dictionary with 'current' and 'highwater' keys as int64 values. Returns an empty Dictionary on error.

to_generate.name: status_memory_used
to_generate.doc: Current amount of memory checked out using sqlite3_malloc().

to_generate.name: status_pagecache_used
to_generate.doc: Number of pages used out of the pagecache memory allocator.

to_generate.name: status_pagecache_overflow
to_generate.doc: Number of bytes of page cache allocation which could not be satisfied by the pagecache buffer.

to_generate.name: status_malloc_size
to_generate.doc: Largest memory allocation request handed to sqlite3_malloc() or sqlite3_realloc().

to_generate.name: status_parser_stack
to_generate.doc: Deepest parser stack. Only meaningful if SQLite is compiled with YYTRACKMAXSTACKDEPTH.

to_generate.name: status_pagecache_size
to_generate.doc: Largest memory allocation request handed to the pagecache memory allocator.

to_generate.name: status_malloc_count
to_generate.doc: Number of separate memory allocations currently checked out.

to_generate.name: db_status
to_generate.doc: Retrieves runtime status information about a single database connection. Returns a Dictionary with 'current' and 'highwater' keys. Returns an empty Dictionary on error.

to_generate.name: db_status64
to_generate.doc: Retrieves runtime status information about a single database connection using 64-bit integers. Returns a Dictionary with 'current' and 'highwater' keys as int64 values. Returns an empty Dictionary on error.

to_generate.name: db_status_lookaside_used
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of lookaside memory slots currently checked out.

to_generate.name: db_status_cache_used
to_generate.doc: Returns the approximate number of bytes of heap memory used by all pager caches associated with the database connection. The highwater mark is always 0.

to_generate.name: db_status_schema_used
to_generate.doc: Returns the approximate number of bytes of heap memory used to store the schema for all databases associated with the connection. The highwater mark is always 0.

to_generate.name: db_status_stmt_used
to_generate.doc: Returns the approximate number of bytes of heap and lookaside memory used by all prepared statements associated with the database connection. The highwater mark is always 0.

to_generate.name: db_status_lookaside_hit
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of malloc attempts that were satisfied using lookaside memory. Only the high-water value is meaningful; the current value is always zero.

to_generate.name: db_status_lookaside_miss_size
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of malloc attempts that might have been satisfied using lookaside memory but failed due to the amount of memory requested being larger than the lookaside slot size. Only the high-water value is meaningful; the current value is always zero.

to_generate.name: db_status_lookaside_miss_full
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of malloc attempts that might have been satisfied using lookaside memory but failed due to all lookaside memory already being in use. Only the high-water value is meaningful; the current value is always zero.

to_generate.name: db_status_cache_hit
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of pager cache hits that have occurred. The highwater mark is always 0.

to_generate.name: db_status_cache_miss
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of pager cache misses that have occurred. The highwater mark is always 0.

to_generate.name: db_status_cache_write
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of dirty cache entries that have been written to disk. The highwater mark is always 0.

to_generate.name: db_status_deferred_fks
to_generate.doc: Returns zero for the current value if and only if all foreign key constraints have been resolved. The highwater mark is always 0.

to_generate.name: db_status_cache_used_shared
to_generate.doc: Returns the approximate number of bytes of heap memory used by all pager caches, divided evenly among shared connections. The highwater mark is always 0.

to_generate.name: db_status_cache_spill
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of dirty cache entries that have been written to disk in the middle of a transaction. The highwater mark is always 0.

to_generate.name: db_status_tempbuf_spill
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of bytes written to temporary files on disk that could have been kept in memory. The highwater mark is always 0.
A wrapper class for the SQLite prepared statement handle.

to_generate.name: stmt_status
to_generate.doc: Retrieves and optionally resets counter values from a prepared statement. The op parameter specifies which counter to query, and reset_flag determines whether to reset the counter after reading. Returns the current value of the requested counter.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_fullscan_step
to_generate.doc: This is the number of times that SQLite has stepped forward in a table as part of a full table scan. Large numbers for this counter may indicate opportunities for performance improvement through careful use of indices.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_sort
to_generate.doc: This is the number of sort operations that have occurred. A non-zero value in this counter may indicate an opportunity to improve performance through careful use of indices.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_autoindex
to_generate.doc: This is the number of rows inserted into transient indices that were created automatically in order to help joins run faster. A non-zero value in this counter may indicate an opportunity to improve performance by adding permanent indices that do not need to be reinitialized each time the statement is run.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_vm_step
to_generate.doc: This is the number of virtual machine operations executed by the prepared statement if that number is less than or equal to 2147483647. The number of virtual machine operations can be used as a proxy for the total work done by the prepared statement. If the number of virtual machine operations exceeds 2147483647 then the value returned by this statement status code is undefined.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_reprepare
to_generate.doc: This is the number of times that the prepare statement has been automatically regenerated due to schema changes or changes to bound parameters that might affect the query plan.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_run
to_generate.doc: This is the number of times that the prepared statement has been run. A single "run" for the purposes of this counter is one or more calls to sqlite3_step() followed by a call to sqlite3_reset(). The counter is incremented on the first sqlite3_step() call of each cycle.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_filter_miss
to_generate.doc: SQLITE_STMTSTATUS_FILTER_HIT is the number of times that a join step was bypassed because a Bloom filter returned not-found. The corresponding SQLITE_STMTSTATUS_FILTER_MISS value is the number of times that the Bloom filter returned a find, and thus the join step had to be processed as normal.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_filter_hit
to_generate.doc: SQLITE_STMTSTATUS_FILTER_HIT is the number of times that a join step was bypassed because a Bloom filter returned not-found. The corresponding SQLITE_STMTSTATUS_FILTER_MISS value is the number of times that the Bloom filter returned a find, and thus the join step had to be processed as normal.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_memused
to_generate.doc: This is the approximate number of bytes of heap memory used to store the prepared statement. This value is not actually a counter, and so the resetFlg parameter to sqlite3_stmt_status() is ignored when the opcode is SQLITE_STMTSTATUS_MEMUSED.
