to_generate.name: str_nicmp
to_generate.doc: Compares up to the first n characters of two UTF-8 strings in a case-independent fashion, using the same definition of case independence that SQLite uses internally for identifiers.

to_generate.name: str_glob
to_generate.doc: Performs glob pattern matching where pattern is the first argument and string is the second. Returns zero if the string matches the glob pattern, non-zero otherwise. This function is case-sensitive and uses the same definition of glob pattern matching as SQLite's 'X GLOB P' operator.

to_generate.name: str_like
to_generate.doc: Performs LIKE pattern matching where pattern is the first argument and string is the second. Returns zero if the string matches the LIKE pattern, non-zero otherwise. This function is case-insensitive (ASCII characters only) and uses the same definition of LIKE pattern matching as SQLite's 'X LIKE P ESCAPE E' operator. Set escape to 0 for patterns without an ESCAPE clause.

to_generate.name: log
to_generate.doc: Writes a message to the SQLite error log. Takes an error code and a format string (printf-style). Note: Variadic arguments are not directly supported in this wrapper; prepare the formatted string in GDScript first using String::format() or similar before calling this function.

to_generate.name: wal_hook
to_generate.doc: Registers a callback to be invoked each time data is committed to a database in WAL mode. The callback receives three arguments: the database handle, the database name ("main" or ATTACH-ed database name), and the number of pages in the WAL file. The callback should return an SQLite error code (typically SQLITE_OK for success). Returns the previous callback's user data as int64_t, or 0 if none was set.

to_generate.name: wal_autocheckpoint
to_generate.doc: Configures automatic checkpointing for a database connection in WAL mode. The database will automatically checkpoint after committing a transaction if there are 'threshold' or more frames in the write-ahead log file. Pass zero or a negative value to disable automatic checkpoints. This replaces any existing callback registered using sqlite3_wal_hook(). Returns an SQLite error code (SQLITE_OK on success).

to_generate.name: wal_checkpoint
to_generate.doc: Performs a checkpoint on a database in WAL mode. This transfers content from the write-ahead log (WAL) into the database file and resets the WAL. Equivalent to sqlite3_wal_checkpoint_v2() with passive mode. Returns an SQLite error code (SQLITE_OK on success).

to_generate.name: wal_checkpoint_v2
to_generate.doc: Runs a checkpoint operation on a database in WAL mode with specified mode. Returns an Array with [error_code, wal_log_size, total_ckpt_frames]. The mode parameter should be one of SQLITE_CHECKPOINT_PASSIVE, FULL, RESTART, TRUNCATE, or NOOP. For database_name, pass empty string or null to checkpoint all attached databases.

to_generate.name: checkpoint_noop
to_generate.doc: Do no work at all

to_generate.name: checkpoint_passive
to_generate.doc: Do as much as possible without blocking

to_generate.name: checkpoint_full
to_generate.doc: Wait for writers, then checkpoint

to_generate.name: checkpoint_restart
to_generate.doc: Like FULL but wait for readers

to_generate.name: checkpoint_truncate
to_generate.doc: Like RESTART but also truncate WAL

to_generate.name: vtab_config
to_generate.doc: Configures virtual table interface. This function may only be called from within xConnect or xCreate methods. Note: Variadic arguments are not supported in this wrapper; only the database handle and operation code are passed. For operations requiring additional parameters, operation-specific wrappers should be implemented.

to_generate.name: vtab_constraint_support
to_generate.doc: Calls of the form sqlite3_vtab_config(db,SQLITE_VTAB_CONSTRAINT_SUPPORT,X) are supported, where X is an integer. If X is zero, then the virtual table does not support constraints. In this configuration (which is the default) if a call to the xUpdate method returns SQLITE_CONSTRAINT, then the entire statement is rolled back as if ON CONFLICT OR ABORT had been specified as part of the user's SQL statement, regardless of the actual ON CONFLICT mode specified.

to_generate.name: vtab_innocuous
to_generate.doc: Calls of the form sqlite3_vtab_config(db,SQLITE_VTAB_INNOCUOUS) from within the xConnect or xCreate methods of a virtual table implementation identify that virtual table as being safe to use from within triggers and views. Conceptually, the SQLITE_VTAB_INNOCUOUS tag means that the virtual table can do no serious harm even if it is controlled by a malicious hacker. Developers should avoid setting the SQLITE_VTAB_INNOCUOUS flag unless absolutely necessary.

to_generate.name: vtab_directonly
to_generate.doc: Calls of the form sqlite3_vtab_config(db,SQLITE_VTAB_DIRECTONLY) from within the xConnect or xCreate methods of a virtual table implementation prohibits that virtual table from being used from within triggers and views.

to_generate.name: vtab_uses_all_schemas
to_generate.doc: Calls of the form sqlite3_vtab_config(db,SQLITE_VTAB_USES_ALL_SCHEMA) from within the xConnect or xCreate methods of a virtual table implementation instruct the query planner to begin at least a read transaction on all schemas ("main", "temp", and any ATTACH-ed databases) whenever the virtual table is used.

to_generate.name: vtab_on_conflict
to_generate.doc: This function may only be called from within a call to the xUpdate method of a virtual table implementation for an INSERT or UPDATE operation. The value returned is one of SQLITE_ROLLBACK, SQLITE_IGNORE, SQLITE_FAIL, SQLITE_ABORT, or SQLITE_REPLACE, according to the ON CONFLICT mode of the SQL statement that triggered the call to the xUpdate method of the virtual table.

to_generate.name: vtab_nochange
to_generate.doc: If the sqlite3_vtab_nochange(X) routine is called within the xColumn method of a virtual table, then it might return true if the column is being fetched as part of an UPDATE operation during which the column value will not change. The virtual table implementation can use this hint as permission to substitute a return value that is less expensive to compute and that the corresponding xUpdate method understands as a "no-change" value.

to_generate.name: vtab_collation
to_generate.doc: This function may only be called from within a call to the xBestIndex method of a virtual table. This function returns a pointer to a string that is the name of the appropriate collation sequence to use for text comparisons on the constraint identified by its arguments. The first parameter must be the pointer to the sqlite3_index_info object that is the first parameter to the xBestIndex() method. The second argument must be an index into the aConstraint[] array belonging to the sqlite3_index_info structure passed to xBestIndex. The return value is computed as follows: (1) If the constraint comes from a WHERE clause expression that contains a COLLATE operator, then the name of the collation specified by that COLLATE operator is returned. (2) If there is no COLLATE operator, but the column that is the subject of the constraint specifies an alternative collating sequence via a COLLATE clause on the column definition within the CREATE TABLE statement that was passed into sqlite3_declare_vtab(), then the name of that alternative collating sequence is returned. (3) Otherwise, "BINARY" is returned.

to_generate.name: vtab_distinct
to_generate.doc: This API may only be used from within an xBestIndex method of a virtual table implementation. The result of calling this interface from outside of xBestIndex() is undefined and probably harmful. ^The sqlite3_vtab_distinct() interface returns an integer between 0 and 3. The integer returned gives the virtual table additional information about how the query planner wants the output to be ordered. Based on the return value, the virtual table may set the "orderByConsumed" flag if it can meet the ordering requirements. Return value 0: rows must be returned in sorted order as defined by nOrderBy and aOrderBy fields. Return value 1: rows don't need to be in sorted order but duplicates must be adjacent (GROUP BY). Return value 2: rows don't need to be in order but duplicates may be omitted (DISTINCT). Return value 3: rows must be in order and duplicates may be omitted (DISTINCT with ORDER BY).

to_generate.name: vtab_in
to_generate.doc: This interface may only be used from within an xBestIndex() method of a virtual table implementation. The result of invoking this interface from any other context is undefined and probably harmful. A constraint on a virtual table of the form [IN operator] is communicated to the xBestIndex method as a SQLITE_INDEX_CONSTRAINT_EQ constraint. The bHandle parameter has three modes: (1) bHandle == -1: query mode - returns true if the constraint can be processed all-at-once; (2) bHandle == 0: tell SQLite not to use all-at-once processing; (3) bHandle >= 1: tell SQLite to use all-at-once processing. For all-at-once processing to be selected, both conditions must be met: (1) aConstraintUsage[].argvIndex is set to a positive integer, and (2) the last call with non-negative bHandle had bHandle >= 1.

to_generate.name: vtab_in_first
to_generate.doc: These interfaces are only useful from within the xFilter() method of a virtual table implementation. The result of invoking these interfaces from any other context is undefined and probably harmful. Use these routines to access all values on the right-hand side of the IN constraint. On success, returns SQLITE_OK and sets out_value to point to the first value on the RHS of the IN constraint. If there are no more values, out_value is set to NULL and returns SQLITE_DONE. The return value might be some other value, such as SQLITE_NOMEM, in the event of a malfunction.

to_generate.name: vtab_in_next
to_generate.doc: These interfaces are only useful from within the xFilter() method of a virtual table implementation. The result of invoking these interfaces from any other context is undefined and probably harmful. Use these routines to access all values on the right-hand side of the IN constraint. On success, returns SQLITE_OK and sets out_value to point to the next value on the RHS of the IN constraint. If there are no more values, out_value is set to NULL and returns SQLITE_DONE. The return value might be some other value, such as SQLITE_NOMEM, in the event of a malfunction. The *ppOut values returned by these routines are only valid until the next call to either of these routines or until the end of the xFilter method from which these routines were called. If the virtual table implementation needs to retain the *ppOut values for longer, it must make copies.

to_generate.name: vtab_rhs_value
to_generate.doc: This API may only be used from within the xBestIndex method of a virtual table implementation. The result of calling this interface from outside of an xBestIndex method are undefined and probably harmful. This routine attempts to set out_value to the value of the right-hand operand of the constraint if the right-hand operand is known. Returns SQLITE_OK if *out_value is set to a value, SQLITE_NOTFOUND if the right-hand side of the constraint is not available, or other result codes if something goes wrong. The _rhs_ in the name is an abbreviation for "Right-Hand Side". The returned sqlite3_value object is protected and remains valid for the duration of the xBestIndex method call, and is automatically deallocated when xBestIndex returns.

to_generate.name: conflict_rollback
to_generate.doc: Returned by sqlite3_vtab_on_conflict() to indicate ON CONFLICT ROLLBACK mode.

to_generate.name: conflict_ignore
to_generate.doc: Returned by sqlite3_vtab_on_conflict() to indicate ON CONFLICT IGNORE mode. Also used by sqlite3_set_authorizer() callback.

to_generate.name: conflict_fail
to_generate.doc: Returned by sqlite3_vtab_on_conflict() to indicate ON CONFLICT FAIL mode.

to_generate.name: conflict_replace
to_generate.doc: Returned by sqlite3_vtab_on_conflict() to indicate ON CONFLICT REPLACE mode.

to_generate.name: stmt_scanstat_nloop
to_generate.doc: The sqlite3_int64 variable pointed to by the V parameter will be set to the total number of times that the X-th loop has run.

to_generate.name: stmt_scanstat_nvisit
to_generate.doc: The sqlite3_int64 variable pointed to by the V parameter will be set to the total number of rows examined by all iterations of the X-th loop.

to_generate.name: stmt_scanstat_est
to_generate.doc: The "double" variable pointed to by the V parameter will be set to the query planner's estimate for the average number of rows output from each iteration of the X-th loop.

to_generate.name: stmt_scanstat_name
to_generate.doc: The "const char *" variable pointed to by the V parameter will be set to a zero-terminated UTF-8 string containing the name of the index or table used for the X-th loop.

to_generate.name: stmt_scanstat_explain
to_generate.doc: The "const char *" variable pointed to by the V parameter will be set to a zero-terminated UTF-8 string containing the EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN description for the X-th loop.

to_generate.name: stmt_scanstat_selectid
to_generate.doc: The "int" variable pointed to by the V parameter will be set to the id for the X-th query plan element. The id value is unique within the statement.

to_generate.name: stmt_scanstat_parentid
to_generate.doc: The "int" variable pointed to by the V parameter will be set to the id of the parent of the current query element, if applicable, or to zero if the query element has no parent.

to_generate.name: stmt_scanstat_ncycle
to_generate.doc: The sqlite3_int64 output value is set to the number of cycles, according to the processor time-stamp counter, that elapsed while the query element was being processed.

to_generate.name: stmt_scanstatus
to_generate.doc: This function returns information about the predicted and measured performance for pStmt. Advanced applications can use this interface to compare the predicted and the measured performance and issue warnings and/or rerun ANALYZE if discrepancies are found. Note: This is a simplified wrapper. The actual function writes to a void* pointer with type depending on scan_status_op. For production use, implement operation-specific wrappers with appropriate type handling.

to_generate.name: stmt_scanstatus_v2
to_generate.doc: This function returns information about the predicted and measured performance for pStmt. The flags parameter controls which query plan elements are available: if SQLITE_SCANSTAT_COMPLEX is specified, all elements are available; otherwise, only query loop elements are available. Note: This is a simplified wrapper. The actual function writes to a void* pointer with type depending on scan_status_op. For production use, implement operation-specific wrappers with appropriate type handling.

to_generate.name: stmt_scanstat_complex
to_generate.doc: Flag for sqlite3_stmt_scanstatus_v2() to indicate that status information is available for all elements of a query plan that are reported by EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN output, not just query loop elements.

to_generate.name: stmt_scanstatus_reset
to_generate.doc: Zero all sqlite3_stmt_scanstatus() related event counters. This API is only available if the library is built with pre-processor symbol SQLITE_ENABLE_STMT_SCANSTATUS defined.

to_generate.name: db_cacheflush
to_generate.doc: If a write-transaction is open on the database connection, any dirty pages in the pager-cache that are not currently in use are written out to disk. Flushes caches for all schemas (main, temp, and attached databases). Returns SQLITE_OK if no error, SQLITE_BUSY if locks cannot be obtained and no other error occurs, or other SQLite error codes if other errors occur. This function does not set the database handle error code or message returned by sqlite3_errcode() and sqlite3_errmsg().

to_generate.name: preupdate_hook
to_generate.doc: Registers a callback function that is invoked prior to each INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE operation on a database table. The callback receives operation type, database name, table name, old rowid, new rowid, old values array, and new values array. This function is only available if SQLite is compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_PREUPDATE_HOOK.

to_generate.name: preupdate_count
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of columns in the row that is being inserted, updated, or deleted. May only be called from within a preupdate callback.

to_generate.name: preupdate_depth
to_generate.doc: Returns 0 if the preupdate callback was invoked as a result of a direct insert, update, or delete operation; or 1 for triggers invoked by top-level triggers; and so forth. May only be called from within a preupdate callback.

to_generate.name: preupdate_old
to_generate.doc: Returns the value of the Nth column of the table row before it is updated. May only be called from within a preupdate callback and only for UPDATE or DELETE operations.

to_generate.name: preupdate_new
to_generate.doc: Returns the value of the Nth column of the table row after it is updated. May only be called from within a preupdate callback and only for INSERT or UPDATE operations.

to_generate.name: preupdate_blobwrite
to_generate.doc: Returns the index of the column being written when sqlite3_blob_write() is used to update a blob column, or -1 for other operations. May only be called from within a preupdate callback.

to_generate.name: system_errno
to_generate.doc: Returns the underlying operating system error code or error number that caused the most recent I/O error or failure to open a file. The return value is OS-dependent (e.g., on Unix systems, this returns the errno value). This function is only meaningful after an I/O error occurs, such as when sqlite3_open_v2() returns SQLITE_CANTOPEN.
A wrapper class for SQLite snapshot objects used in WAL mode to record database state at a specific point in time.

to_generate.name: snapshot_get
to_generate.doc: Attempts to make a new snapshot object capturing the current state of schema S in database connection D. Returns SQLITE_OK on success and writes the snapshot pointer to out_snapshot.

to_generate.name: snapshot_open
to_generate.doc: Starts a read transaction on an historical snapshot. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: snapshot_free
to_generate.doc: Destroys a snapshot object to avoid memory leaks.

to_generate.name: snapshot_cmp
to_generate.doc: Compares the ages of two snapshot handles. Returns negative if p1 is older, zero if same, positive if p1 is newer.

to_generate.name: snapshot_recover
to_generate.doc: Recovers valid snapshots from a WAL file so they can be used with sqlite3_snapshot_open(). Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: serialize
to_generate.doc: Serializes the database to a byte array. Returns a PackedByteArray containing the database contents. Memory is copied and managed by Godot. The flags argument can include SQLITE_SERIALIZE_NOCOPY to avoid allocation (but the data is still copied by this wrapper for safety). Use .size() on the result to get the size.

to_generate.name: serialize_flag_nocopy
to_generate.doc: Flag for sqlite3_serialize() to avoid memory allocation by returning a pointer to existing contiguous memory.

to_generate.name: deserialize
to_generate.doc: Reopens a database from serialized data. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: deserialize_flag_freeonclose
to_generate.doc: Flag for sqlite3_deserialize(): SQLite takes ownership of the serialization buffer and frees it on close.

to_generate.name: deserialize_flag_resizeable
to_generate.doc: Flag for sqlite3_deserialize(): SQLite is allowed to grow the database size using sqlite3_realloc64().

to_generate.name: deserialize_flag_readonly
to_generate.doc: Flag for sqlite3_deserialize(): The deserialized database is treated as read-only.

to_generate.name: carray_bind_v2
to_generate.doc: Binds an array value to a parameter of a prepared statement for use with the carray() table-valued function. Uses SQLITE_STATIC (no copy, no destructor) internally. Advanced use cases requiring custom destructors should use the raw C API.

to_generate.name: carray_bind
to_generate.doc: Binds an array value to a parameter of a prepared statement for use with the carray() table-valued function. Uses no destructor (SQLITE_STATIC behavior). Advanced use cases requiring custom destructors should use the raw C API.

to_generate.name: carray_type_int32
to_generate.doc: Datatype flag for sqlite3_carray_bind(): 32-bit signed integers.

to_generate.name: carray_type_int64
to_generate.doc: Datatype flag for sqlite3_carray_bind(): 64-bit signed integers.

to_generate.name: carray_type_double
to_generate.doc: Datatype flag for sqlite3_carray_bind(): doubles.

to_generate.name: carray_type_text
to_generate.doc: Datatype flag for sqlite3_carray_bind(): char* strings.

to_generate.name: carray_type_blob
to_generate.doc: Datatype flag for sqlite3_carray_bind(): struct iovec (binary data).
A wrapper class for SQLite R-tree geometry handles used in spatial queries.
A wrapper class for SQLite R-tree query info handles used in R-tree callback functions.

to_generate.name: rtree_query_callback
to_generate.doc: Registers an R-tree query callback for spatial queries. The callback receives a query_info handle and must return an integer result code. Returns SQLITE_OK on success.
Wrapper for sqlite3_rtree_geometry used in R-tree geometry callbacks.
Wrapper for sqlite3_rtree_query_info used in R-tree query callbacks. Provides access to coordinates, scores, and visibility flags. Output fields `e_within` and `r_score` can be set by callbacks.

to_generate.name: not_within
to_generate.doc: Object completely outside of query region.

to_generate.name: partly_within
to_generate.doc: Object partially overlaps query region.

to_generate.name: fully_within
to_generate.doc: Object fully contained within query region.
Session Object Handle. An instance of this object is a session that can be used to record changes to a database.
Changeset Iterator Handle. An instance of this object acts as a cursor for iterating over the elements of a changeset or patchset.

to_generate.name: session_create
to_generate.doc: Create a new session object attached to database handle db. If successful, a pointer to the new object is written to out_session and SQLITE_OK is returned. Otherwise, out_session is not modified and an SQLite error code is returned.

to_generate.name: session_delete
to_generate.doc: Delete a session object previously allocated using sqlite3session_create. Once deleted, using the session object with any other session module function results in undefined behavior.

to_generate.name: session_object_config
to_generate.doc: Configure a session object after creation. Valid op codes include SQLITE_SESSION_OBJCONFIG_SIZE and SQLITE_SESSION_OBJCONFIG_ROWID.

to_generate.name: session_objconfig_size
to_generate.doc: Option for sqlite3session_object_config to configure the sqlite3session_changeset_size() API.

to_generate.name: session_objconfig_rowid
to_generate.doc: Option for sqlite3session_object_config to enable collection of data for tables with no explicit PRIMARY KEY.

to_generate.name: session_enable
to_generate.doc: Enable or disable the recording of changes by a session object. Passing zero disables the session, greater than zero enables it, and less than zero is a no-op that queries the current state. Returns 1 if enabled, 0 if disabled.

to_generate.name: session_indirect
to_generate.doc: Set, clear, or query the session object's indirect flag. Passing zero clears the flag, greater than zero sets it, and less than zero queries the current state without modifying it. Returns 1 if the indirect flag is set, 0 if clear.

to_generate.name: session_attach
to_generate.doc: Attach a table to the session object. If z_tab is empty or null, changes are recorded for all tables. Returns SQLITE_OK on success.

to_generate.name: session_table_filter
to_generate.doc: Set a table filter callback on the session object. The filter callback receives the table name and returns 0 to skip tracking or non-zero to track changes. Note that once a table is attached, the filter will not be called again for it.

to_generate.name: session_changeset
to_generate.doc: Obtain a changeset containing changes to attached tables. On success, out_changeset contains the changeset buffer and out_size its size in bytes. The caller is responsible for freeing the buffer via sqlite3_free (handled internally here). Returns SQLITE_OK on success.

to_generate.name: session_changeset_size
to_generate.doc: Return an upper-limit for the size of the changeset in bytes. Only meaningful if SQLITE_SESSION_OBJCONFIG_SIZE was enabled via sqlite3session_object_config(). Returns 0 if disabled or no changes.

to_generate.name: session_diff
to_generate.doc: Load the difference between tables into a session. Compares the table attached to the session with a compatible table in another database and adds INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE records. Returns SQLITE_OK on success. On error, out_error contains the error message.

to_generate.name: session_patchset
to_generate.doc: Generate a patchset from a session object. A patchset is a compact version of a changeset with reduced field data. Returns SQLITE_OK on success. out_patchset contains the patchset buffer and out_size its size in bytes.

to_generate.name: session_isempty
to_generate.doc: Return true if no changes have been recorded by the session object. If one or more changes have been recorded, return false. If this function returns true, a subsequent call to session_changeset() is guaranteed to return an empty changeset.

to_generate.name: session_memory_used
to_generate.doc: Return the total amount of heap memory in bytes currently used by the session object.

to_generate.name: changeset_start
to_generate.doc: Create an iterator to traverse a changeset. On success, out_iter contains the iterator handle. The changeset buffer must remain valid until the iterator is destroyed.

to_generate.name: changeset_start_v2
to_generate.doc: Create an iterator to traverse a changeset with additional flags. On success, out_iter contains the iterator handle. The changeset buffer must remain valid until the iterator is destroyed.

to_generate.name: changeset_start_invert_flag
to_generate.doc: Flag for sqlite3changeset_start_v2 to invert the changeset while iterating. It is an error to specify this flag with a patchset.

to_generate.name: changeset_next
to_generate.doc: Advance a changeset iterator to the next change. Returns SQLITE_ROW if a change is available, SQLITE_DONE if no more changes, or an error code.

to_generate.name: changeset_op
to_generate.doc: Obtain the current operation from a changeset iterator. On success, out_table contains the table name, out_col_count the column count, out_op the operation type (SQLITE_INSERT/DELETE/UPDATE), and out_indirect whether the change is indirect.

to_generate.name: changeset_pk
to_generate.doc: Obtain the primary key definition of the table modified by the current change. On success, out_pk contains a boolean array (1 for PK columns, 0 otherwise) and out_col_count the number of columns.

to_generate.name: changeset_old
to_generate.doc: Obtain an old.* value from a changeset iterator. For DELETE or UPDATE changes, returns the i_val-th original column value. On success, out_value contains the sqlite3_value handle.

to_generate.name: changeset_new
to_generate.doc: Obtain a new.* value from a changeset iterator. For UPDATE or INSERT changes, returns the i_val-th new column value. If the change does not include a new value for the column, out_value is set to null. On success, out_value contains the sqlite3_value handle.

to_generate.name: changeset_conflict
to_generate.doc: Obtain a conflicting row value from a changeset iterator. For UPDATE or INSERT changes, returns the i_val-th value from the conflicting row. Only valid for iterators passed to conflict-handler callbacks with SQLITE_CHANGESET_DATA or SQLITE_CHANGESET_CONFLICT. Returns SQLITE_OK on success and sets out_value to the conflicting value.

to_generate.name: changeset_fk_conflicts
to_generate.doc: Determine the number of foreign key constraint violations. May only be called with an iterator passed to an SQLITE_CHANGESET_FOREIGN_KEY conflict handler callback. Returns an Array with [error_code, fk_count]. On success, error_code is SQLITE_OK and fk_count is the total number of known foreign key violations.

to_generate.name: changeset_finalize
to_generate.doc: Finalize a changeset iterator allocated with sqlite3changeset_start(). Should only be called on iterators created using sqlite3changeset_start(). If called on iterators passed to conflict handlers, returns SQLITE_MISUSE immediately.

to_generate.name: changeset_invert
to_generate.doc: Invert a changeset. Reverses the effects of applying the uninverted changeset: DELETE changes become INSERT, INSERT become DELETE, and UPDATE old/new values are exchanged. Returns an Array with [error_code, inverted_changeset]. The caller is responsible for memory management of the output buffer via SQLite's sqlite3_free().

to_generate.name: changeset_concat
to_generate.doc: Concatenate two changesets, A and B, into a single changeset equivalent to applying A followed by B. Returns an Array with [error_code, concatenated_changeset]. The caller is responsible for memory management of the output buffer via SQLite's sqlite3_free().
A wrapper class for SQLite's changegroup handle, used to combine two or more changesets or patchsets.

to_generate.name: changegroup_new
to_generate.doc: Create a new changegroup object. A changegroup is used to combine two or more changesets or patchsets into a single changeset. Returns SQLITE_OK on success and sets out_handle to the new changegroup handle. The caller must eventually free the handle using sqlite3changegroup_delete().

to_generate.name: changegroup_schema
to_generate.doc: Add a schema to a changegroup to enforce compatibility with database zDb. If sqlite3changegroup_add() is called with changesets that are not compatible with the configured schema, SQLITE_SCHEMA is returned and the changegroup object is left in an undefined state.

to_generate.name: changegroup_add
to_generate.doc: Add all changes within the changeset (or patchset) in buffer input to the changegroup. All prior calls to this function on the same changegroup object must also have specified patchsets (or all changesets). Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code if an error occurs.

to_generate.name: changegroup_add_change
to_generate.doc: Add the single change currently indicated by the iterator to the changegroup. The iterator must point to a valid entry and must not have been opened with the SQLITE_CHANGESETAPPLY_INVERT flag. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an SQLite error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: changegroup_output
to_generate.doc: Obtain a buffer containing a changeset (or patchset) representing the current contents of the changegroup. Returns an Array with [error_code, output_changeset]. The caller is responsible for memory management of the output buffer via sqlite3_free().

to_generate.name: changeset_apply
to_generate.doc: Apply a changeset to a database. The filter_callback determines which tables to apply changes to (optional, pass empty Callable for all tables). The conflict_callback handles conflicts during application (required). Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: changeset_apply_v2
to_generate.doc: Apply a changeset to a database with additional control via flags. The filter_callback determines which tables to apply changes to (optional, pass empty Callable for all tables). The conflict_callback handles conflicts during application (required). The rebase_data output parameter receives rebased data if conflicts occurred and the changeset is not a patchset. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: changeset_apply_v3
to_generate.doc: Apply a changeset to a database with v3 semantics. The filter_callback receives an iterator handle for the current change instead of just a table name, allowing more sophisticated filtering. The conflict_callback handles conflicts during application (required). The rebase_data output parameter receives rebased data if conflicts occurred and the changeset is not a patchset. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: changeset_apply_flag_no_savepoint
to_generate.doc: Flag for sqlite3changeset_apply_v2/v3: Do not enclose operations in a savepoint.

to_generate.name: changeset_apply_flag_invert
to_generate.doc: Flag for sqlite3changeset_apply_v2/v3: Invert the changeset before applying it.

to_generate.name: changeset_apply_flag_ignore_noop
to_generate.doc: Flag for sqlite3changeset_apply_v2/v3: Do not invoke conflict handler for changes that would not modify the database.

to_generate.name: changeset_apply_flag_fk_no_action
to_generate.doc: Flag for sqlite3changeset_apply_v2/v3: Foreign keys behave as if declared with ON UPDATE/DELETE NO ACTION.

to_generate.name: changeset_conflict_type_data
to_generate.doc: Conflict type: Non-primary key fields don't contain expected 'before' values.

to_generate.name: changeset_conflict_type_not_found
to_generate.doc: Conflict type: Row with required primary key fields not present in database.

to_generate.name: changeset_conflict_type_conflict
to_generate.doc: Conflict type: INSERT would result in duplicate primary key values.

to_generate.name: changeset_conflict_type_constraint
to_generate.doc: Conflict type: Other constraint violation (UNIQUE, CHECK, NOT NULL).

to_generate.name: changeset_conflict_type_foreign_key
to_generate.doc: Conflict type: Foreign key constraint violation after applying changeset.

to_generate.name: changeset_conflict_result_omit
to_generate.doc: Conflict handler return value: Skip the conflicting change.

to_generate.name: changeset_conflict_result_replace
to_generate.doc: Conflict handler return value: Replace the conflicting row (only for DATA or CONFLICT conflicts).

to_generate.name: changeset_conflict_result_abort
to_generate.doc: Conflict handler return value: Roll back all changes and abort.
A wrapper class for the SQLite rebaser handle used for rebasing changesets.

to_generate.name: rebaser_create
to_generate.doc: Creates a new sqlite3_rebaser object. Returns a Ref to the handle, or an empty Ref on error.

to_generate.name: rebaser_configure
to_generate.doc: Configures the rebaser with rebase data from sqlite3changeset_apply_v2(). Can be called multiple times to configure with multiple rebase buffers.

to_generate.name: rebaser_rebase
to_generate.doc: Rebases a changeset using the configured rebase data. Returns the rebased changeset as a PackedByteArray, or an empty array on error.

to_generate.name: rebaser_destroy
to_generate.doc: Destroys the sqlite3_rebaser object and frees associated resources.

to_generate.name: changeset_apply_stream
to_generate.doc: Streaming version of changeset_apply. Takes a PackedByteArray for the changeset and applies it to the database. The filter_callback determines which tables to apply changes to (optional). The conflict_callback handles conflicts during application (required). Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: changeset_apply_v2_stream
to_generate.doc: Streaming version of changeset_apply_v2. Takes a PackedByteArray for the changeset and applies it to the database. The filter_callback determines which tables to apply changes to (optional). The conflict_callback handles conflicts during application (required). The rebase_data output parameter receives rebased data if conflicts occurred. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: changeset_apply_v3_stream
to_generate.doc: Streaming version of changeset_apply_v3. Takes a PackedByteArray for the changeset and applies it to the database. The filter_callback receives an iterator handle for the current change instead of just a table name, allowing more sophisticated filtering. The conflict_callback handles conflicts during application (required). The rebase_data output parameter receives rebased data if conflicts occurred. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: changeset_concat_stream
to_generate.doc: Streaming version of changeset_concat. Concatenates two changesets into one. Returns the concatenated changeset as a PackedByteArray, or an empty array on error.

to_generate.name: changeset_invert_stream
to_generate.doc: Streaming version of changeset_invert. Inverts a changeset (converts INSERTs to DELETEs and vice versa, swaps UPDATE old/new values). Returns the inverted changeset as a PackedByteArray, or an empty array on error.

to_generate.name: changeset_start_stream
to_generate.doc: Streaming version of changeset_start. Starts iterating over a changeset. Returns a Ref to the iterator handle, or an empty Ref on error.

to_generate.name: changeset_start_v2_stream
to_generate.doc: Streaming version of changeset_start_v2. Starts iterating over a changeset with configuration flags. Returns a Ref to the iterator handle, or an empty Ref on error.

to_generate.name: session_changeset_stream
to_generate.doc: Streaming version of session_changeset. Extracts a changeset from a session. Returns the changeset as a PackedByteArray, or an empty array on error.

to_generate.name: session_patchset_stream
to_generate.doc: Streaming version of session_patchset. Extracts a patchset from a session. Returns the patchset as a PackedByteArray, or an empty array on error.

to_generate.name: changegroup_add_stream
to_generate.doc: Streaming version of changegroup_add. Adds changes from a PackedByteArray to a changegroup. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code on failure.

to_generate.name: changegroup_output_stream
to_generate.doc: Streaming version of changegroup_output. Extracts a changeset from a changegroup. Returns the changeset as a PackedByteArray, or an empty array on error.

to_generate.name: rebaser_rebase_stream
to_generate.doc: Streaming version of rebaser_rebase. Rebases a changeset using the rebaser. Returns the rebased changeset as a PackedByteArray, or an empty array on error.

to_generate.name: session_config_stream_size
to_generate.doc: Configures the streaming data chunk size for the sessions module. Returns the final chunk size on success, or an SQLite error code on failure. Note: This function is not threadsafe and must not be called while any other thread is inside sessions methods or after any sessions objects have been created.

to_generate.name: session_config_strmsize
to_generate.doc: Operation code for sqlite3session_config() to configure streaming data chunk size.

A wrapper class for the FTS5 context handle used in custom auxiliary functions.
